 {"id":"bd-1323","title":"E2E Tests: Dashboard visual regression with VHS recordings","description":"## Overview
End-to-end visual regression tests using VHS (terminal recording) to verify the dashboard renders correctly across different scenarios.

## Prerequisites
- VHS installed: \`brew install vhs\` or \`go install github.com/charmbracelet/vhs@latest\`
- ffmpeg for video processing
- ImageMagick for screenshot comparison

## Test File Location
- tests/e2e/dashboard_visual_test.go
- testdata/vhs/*.tape (VHS tape files)
- testdata/golden/*.png (golden screenshots)

## VHS Tape Files

### 1. Basic Render Test
\`\`\`tape
# File: testdata/vhs/dashboard-basic.tape
Output testdata/vhs/dashboard-basic.gif
Set FontSize 14
Set Width 1200
Set Height 800
Set Theme "Catppuccin Mocha"
Set TypingSpeed 50ms

# Start dashboard with test session
Type "ntm dashboard test-session"
Enter
Sleep 3s

# Take screenshot after initial render
Screenshot testdata/screenshots/basic-initial.png

# Verify no duplication by checking status bar area
Sleep 1s
Screenshot testdata/screenshots/basic-stable.png
\`\`\`

### 2. Resize Test
\`\`\`tape
# File: testdata/vhs/dashboard-resize.tape
Output testdata/vhs/dashboard-resize.gif
Set FontSize 14
Set Width 1200
Set Height 800
Set Theme "Catppuccin Mocha"

Type "ntm dashboard test-session"
Enter
Sleep 2s
Screenshot testdata/screenshots/resize-wide.png

# Narrow terminal
Set Width 800
Sleep 1s
Screenshot testdata/screenshots/resize-narrow.png

# Very narrow
Set Width 600
Sleep 1s
Screenshot testdata/screenshots/resize-compact.png

# Back to wide
Set Width 1600
Sleep 1s
Screenshot testdata/screenshots/resize-ultrawide.png
\`\`\`

### 3. Navigation Test
\`\`\`tape
# File: testdata/vhs/dashboard-navigation.tape
Output testdata/vhs/dashboard-navigation.gif
Set FontSize 14
Set Width 1200
Set Height 800

Type "ntm dashboard test-session"
Enter
Sleep 2s

# Navigate down
Type "j"
Sleep 300ms
Screenshot testdata/screenshots/nav-down1.png

Type "j"
Sleep 300ms
Type "j"
Sleep 300ms
Screenshot testdata/screenshots/nav-down3.png

# Navigate up
Type "k"
Sleep 300ms
Screenshot testdata/screenshots/nav-up.png

# Select by number
Type "5"
Sleep 500ms
Screenshot testdata/screenshots/nav-select5.png
\`\`\`

### 4. Refresh Test (No Accumulation)
\`\`\`tape
# File: testdata/vhs/dashboard-refresh.tape
Output testdata/vhs/dashboard-refresh.gif
Set FontSize 14
Set Width 1200
Set Height 800

Type "ntm dashboard test-session"
Enter
Sleep 2s
Screenshot testdata/screenshots/refresh-before.png

# Manual refresh
Type "r"
Sleep 2s
Screenshot testdata/screenshots/refresh-after1.png

Type "r"
Sleep 2s
Screenshot testdata/screenshots/refresh-after2.png

Type "r"
Sleep 2s
Screenshot testdata/screenshots/refresh-after3.png
\`\`\`

## Visual Comparison Script
\`\`\`bash
#!/bin/bash
# scripts/visual-regression.sh

set -euo pipefail

TESTDATA="testdata"
GOLDEN="$TESTDATA/golden"
SCREENSHOTS="$TESTDATA/screenshots"
THRESHOLD=100  # Maximum pixel difference allowed

echo "Running VHS tapes..."
for tape in $TESTDATA/vhs/*.tape; do
    echo "  Running: $tape"
    vhs "$tape" 2>&1 | tee -a vhs.log
done

echo "Comparing screenshots..."
FAILED=0
for golden in $GOLDEN/*.png; do
    name=$(basename "$golden")
    screenshot="$SCREENSHOTS/$name"
    
    if [[ ! -f "$screenshot" ]]; then
        echo "  MISSING: $name"
        FAILED=1
        continue
    fi
    
    # Compare with ImageMagick
    diff=$(compare -metric AE "$golden" "$screenshot" /dev/null 2>&1 || true)
    
    if [[ "$diff" -gt "$THRESHOLD" ]]; then
        echo "  FAIL: $name (diff: $diff pixels)"
        # Generate diff image for debugging
        compare "$golden" "$screenshot" "$TESTDATA/diff-$name" || true
        FAILED=1
    else
        echo "  PASS: $name (diff: $diff pixels)"
    fi
done

if [[ "$FAILED" -eq 1 ]]; then
    echo ""
    echo "Visual regression tests FAILED"
    echo "Check testdata/diff-*.png for differences"
    exit 1
fi

echo ""
echo "All visual regression tests PASSED"
\`\`\`

## E2E Test Wrapper
\`\`\`go
// tests/e2e/dashboard_visual_test.go
func TestDashboard_VisualRegression(t *testing.T) {
    if os.Getenv("RUN_VISUAL_TESTS") == "" {
        t.Skip("set RUN_VISUAL_TESTS=1 to run visual regression tests")
    }
    
    // Run visual regression script
    cmd := exec.Command("bash", "scripts/visual-regression.sh")
    cmd.Dir = projectRoot()
    output, err := cmd.CombinedOutput()
    
    t.Log(string(output))
    
    if err != nil {
        t.Fatalf("visual regression failed: %v", err)
    }
}
\`\`\`

## Specific Assertions

### No Status Bar Duplication
Check that screenshots contain exactly one instance of status bar text.

### Proper Layout at All Sizes
- 80x24: Compact mode, essential elements visible
- 120x40: Standard mode, all panels visible
- 200x60: Wide mode, expanded layout

### Animation Stability
Multiple screenshots at same state should be visually identical (except for animations).

## CI Integration
\`\`\`yaml
visual-tests:
  runs-on: ubuntu-latest
  steps:
    - uses: actions/checkout@v4
    - name: Install VHS
      run: |
        brew install vhs imagemagick ffmpeg
    - name: Run visual tests
      run: |
        RUN_VISUAL_TESTS=1 go test -v ./tests/e2e/...
    - name: Upload diff images
      if: failure()
      uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
      with:
        name: visual-diffs
        path: testdata/diff-*.png
\`\`\`

## Updating Golden Files
\`\`\`bash
# After intentional UI changes, update golden files:
UPDATE_GOLDEN=1 RUN_VISUAL_TESTS=1 go test -v ./tests/e2e/...

# Or manually:
cp testdata/screenshots/*.png testdata/golden/
\`\`\`

## Logging
All test runs should log:
- VHS version
- Terminal size for each tape
- Screenshot file paths
- Pixel difference for each comparison
- Full command output on failure","notes":"VHS tape files created in testdata/vhs/. E2E test wrapper in tests/e2e/dashboard_visual_test.go. Visual regression script at scripts/visual-regression.sh. Directories testdata/golden and testdata/screenshots created. Golden images need to be generated when VHS is installed. Infrastructure is complete, ready to generate golden images with: UPDATE_GOLDEN=1 go test -v -run TestUpdateGoldenImages ./tests/e2e","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"ubuntu","created_at":"2026-01-19T07:13:09.085341313Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-20T06:15:57.067104553Z","closed_at":"2026-01-20T06:15:57.067005807Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1323","depends_on_id":"bd-36he","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:24Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1323","depends_on_id":"bd-3b3f","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:24Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-1370q","title":"EPIC: Support Bundle (Diagnostics + Safe Sharing)","description":"## Summary
Add a first-class way to generate **safe, shareable diagnostics** for NTM issues.

Today users often paste logs/pane output manually (high leak risk, low reproducibility). A support bundle command can capture:
- environment + tool versions
- effective config
- recent event log slice
- relevant session snapshot(s)
- optional pane excerpts
…while applying redaction and respecting privacy mode.

## Goals
- One command produces a single archive file (`.zip` or `.tar.gz`).
- Safe-by-default: redaction enabled by default; explicit overrides required.
- Deterministic manifest with checksums for integrity.

## Non-goals
- Uploading bundles to third-party services (keep local; users can share manually).

## Success Criteria
- Bundle generation works without running sessions (still includes env/config).
- Works in robot mode with stable JSON output.
- Includes enough info to reproduce common bugs (tmux base-index, config parsing, etc.).
- Comprehensive unit + E2E tests with detailed logs.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:26:55.667286751Z","created_by":"OrangeWolf","updated_at":"2026-02-01T03:26:55.667286751Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-13rct","title":"Task: Implement EarlyStopDetector for marginal utility stopping","description":"# Task: EarlyStopDetector Implementation

Stop ensemble when new findings rate drops below threshold.

## Implementation
```go
// internal/ensemble/early_stop.go
type EarlyStopConfig struct {
    MinAgentsBeforeStop int     // Run at least N agents first
    FindingsThreshold   float64 // Stop if findings/token < threshold
    SimilarityThreshold float64 // Stop if output similarity > threshold
    WindowSize          int     // Evaluate over last N agents
    Enabled             bool
}

type EarlyStopDetector struct {
    Config        EarlyStopConfig
    Outputs       []ModeOutput
    TokensSpent   []int
}

type StopDecision struct {
    ShouldStop    bool
    Reason        string
    FindingsRate  float64
    SimilarityScore float64
    AgentsRun     int
}

func NewEarlyStopDetector(cfg EarlyStopConfig) *EarlyStopDetector
func (d *EarlyStopDetector) RecordOutput(output ModeOutput, tokens int)
func (d *EarlyStopDetector) ShouldStop() StopDecision
func (d *EarlyStopDetector) CalculateFindingsRate() float64
func (d *EarlyStopDetector) CalculateSimilarity() float64
```

## Stopping Criteria

### Findings Rate Drop
```
Rate = (new unique findings in window) / (tokens spent in window)
Stop if: rate < threshold AND agents_run >= min_agents
```

### Output Similarity
```
Similarity = average pairwise similarity of outputs in window
Stop if: similarity > threshold (outputs are redundant)
```

## Logging
- Log each decision with metrics
- Log when approaching threshold
- Log final stop reason

 {"id":"bd-3izi","title":"Add ntm watch --bead to monitor progress on a specific bead across all agents","description":"Add ntm watch --bead to monitor progress on a specific bead across all agents.

## Problem Being Solved

When multiple agents work on related beads, tracking progress on a specific bead is difficult.

## Command Design

```bash
ntm watch SESSION --bead BEAD_ID [--interval DURATION]
```

### Behavior
1. Monitors all agent panes for mentions of BEAD_ID
2. Shows real-time progress updates
3. Correlates with bv status for that bead

### Output
```
Watching bead bd-abc123 across 5 agents...

[10:30:15] cc:2 mentioned bd-abc123: "Starting work on bd-abc123"
[10:32:00] cc:2 mentioned bd-abc123: "Implementing feature..."
[10:35:00] cod:4 mentioned bd-abc123: "Reviewing changes for bd-abc123"
[10:38:00] bv status: bd-abc123 -> DONE
```

### Options
- --interval: How often to check bv status (default: 30s)

## Robot Mode API
ntm --robot-watch-bead=SESSION --bead=BEAD_ID

Returns JSON:
```json
{
  "bead_id": "bd-abc123",
  "mentions": [
    {"pane": 2, "timestamp": "...", "line": "..."}
  ],
  "bead_status": "in_progress"
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria

1. ntm watch --bead flag added to watch command
2. Monitors all panes for bead ID mentions
3. Real-time output shows pane, timestamp, and context
4. Integrates with bv to show bead status changes
5. --interval configures bv polling frequency
6. Robot mode returns JSON with mentions array

## Unit Tests (80% coverage target)
- internal/cmd/watch_test.go
- Test bead ID pattern matching
- Test bv integration

## E2E Test Script
```bash
#!/bin/bash
# [E2E-WATCH-BEAD] Watch Bead Tests
LOG_PREFIX="[E2E-WATCH-BEAD]"
echo "$LOG_PREFIX Testing ntm watch --bead"
```","acceptance_criteria":"1. ntm watch --bead BEAD_ID monitors progress
2. Shows activity across all agents on that bead
3. Live updates when agents mention bead
4. Highlights commits and file changes
5. JSON output in robot mode
6. Unit tests for bead matching
7. E2E test with real agent activity","notes":"## Testing Requirements
- Unit tests: core logic, error paths, edge cases.
- E2E scripts: full CLI/robot flow with detailed log/slog prefixes (e.g., [E2E-<AREA>]) and include session/agent/bead IDs.
- If structured outputs are affected, assert schema stability + deterministic ordering.

 {"id":"bd-3jbum","title":"Feature: Tool inventory + health in robot mode","description":"## Overview
Expose a stable robot-first view of tool inventory and health status using the tools registry.

## Deliverables
- `ntm --robot-tools` (or `ntm tools --json`) with full ToolInfo list
- Include tools in `ntm --robot-snapshot` output
- Extend `ntm deps`/`ntm doctor` to show missing/optional tools
- Include JFP and TOON if adapters are available
- Surface extra status fields when available (e.g., DCG guard active)

## Output Shape (robot)
```json
{
  "success": true,
  "tools": [
    {"name":"bv","installed":true,"version":"...","capabilities":[...],"health":{...}},
    {"name":"dcg","installed":false,"health":{"message":"not installed"},"required":false},
    {"name":"jfp","installed":true,"version":"..."}
  ]
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- Output is stable for automation
- Missing tools are clearly reported

 {"id":"bd-3jxy","title":"Fix list item deduplication in activity panel","description":"## Problem
The activity panel shows entries that APPEAR duplicated but may actually be different panes with similar names that become indistinguishable after truncation.

## Screenshot Evidence Analysis
Left panel shows approximately 17 rows all displaying "destructive_command_guar..." with different prefixes (★, blank).

**Key insight:** These are likely DIFFERENT panes:
- `destructive_command_guard_cc_1`
- `destructive_command_guard_cc_2`
- ...
- `destructive_command_guard_cc_17`

When truncated with end-ellipsis, they ALL become:
- `destructive_command_guar...` (INDISTINGUISHABLE)

## Root Cause: TWO Issues

### Issue A: Actual Duplicates (if present)
If the same entry truly appears multiple times, this is a data/render bug.

### Issue B: Truncation Making Different Items Look Identical (MORE LIKELY)
The TruncateRunes function cuts from the end, removing the distinguishing suffix.
**This is now fixed by bd-2qcn** which implements TruncateMiddle.

## Fix Implementation

### For Issue A: Deduplication
\`\`\`go
func (p *ActivityPanel) SetData(activities []Activity) {
    // Deduplicate by unique key
    seen := make(map[string]bool)
    unique := make([]Activity, 0, len(activities))
    for _, a := range activities {
        key := fmt.Sprintf("%s:%s", a.PaneID, a.Timestamp)
        if !seen[key] {
            seen[key] = true
            unique = append(unique, a)
        }
    }
    p.list.SetItems(unique)
}
\`\`\`

### For Issue B: Smart Truncation (PRIMARY FIX)
Use the new TruncateMiddle function from bd-2qcn:
\`\`\`go
func (p *ActivityPanel) renderItem(item Activity) string {
    // Use middle truncation to preserve distinguishing suffix
    name := layout.TruncateMiddle(item.Name, p.nameWidth)
    // Result: "destru…_cc_1", "destru…_cc_2" (DISTINGUISHABLE!)
    return name
}
\`\`\`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] No TRUE duplicates (same pane ID appears once)
- [ ] Different panes with similar names are VISUALLY DISTINGUISHABLE
- [ ] Items are properly ordered (most recent first)
- [ ] Refresh doesn't cause duplication

## Unit Tests
\`\`\`go
func TestActivityPanel_NoDuplicates(t *testing.T) {
    panel := panels.NewActivityPanel()
    
    // Add activities with same ID (should dedupe)
    activities := []Activity{
        {ID: "pane_1", Title: "Task A"},
        {ID: "pane_1", Title: "Task A"}, // Duplicate
        {ID: "pane_2", Title: "Task B"},
    }
    
    panel.SetData(activities)
    
    // Should have 2 items, not 3
    if panel.ItemCount() != 2 {
        t.Errorf("expected 2 items after dedup, got %d", panel.ItemCount())
    }
}

func TestActivityPanel_SimilarNamesDistinguishable(t *testing.T) {
    panel := panels.NewActivityPanel()
    panel.SetSize(40, 20)
    
    // Add activities with similar names
    activities := []Activity{
        {ID: "1", Title: "destructive_command_guard_cc_1"},
        {ID: "2", Title: "destructive_command_guard_cc_2"},
        {ID: "16", Title: "destructive_command_guard_cc_16"},
    }
    
    panel.SetData(activities)
    view := panel.View()
    
    // Each truncated name should be unique in the view
    lines := strings.Split(view, "\n")
    seen := make(map[string]bool)
    for _, line := range lines {
        trimmed := strings.TrimSpace(line)
        if trimmed == "" || !strings.Contains(trimmed, "destru") {
            continue
        }
        if seen[trimmed] {
            t.Errorf("duplicate line in view: %q", trimmed)
        }
        seen[trimmed] = true
    }
}
\`\`\`

## E2E Tests
1. Launch dashboard with multiple similar-named panes - verify they're distinguishable
2. Spawn new pane - verify it appears once
3. Kill pane - verify it's removed
4. Rapid refresh - verify no accumulation

## Logging
\`\`\`go
log.Debug("activity panel rendering",
    "total_items", len(activities),
    "deduped_count", len(unique),
    "duplicates_removed", len(activities) - len(unique))

// Log if similar names detected
if hasSimilarNames(unique) {
    log.Debug("similar names detected, using middle truncation")
}
\`\`\`

## Dependencies
- **bd-2qcn** (TruncateMiddle) - PRIMARY dependency for making similar items distinguishable
- **bd-1xoe** (content height) - Ensures panel content doesn't overflow","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T07:08:18.819688166Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-19T10:09:04.991439382Z","closed_at":"2026-01-19T09:04:48.516792842Z","close_reason":"Fixed visually identical pane titles by implementing TruncatePaneTitle() in layout.go that preserves agent suffix (__cc_1, __gmi_2, etc.) when truncating. Panes with the same project prefix now remain distinguishable. Also cleaned up duplicate terminal package code.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3jxy","depends_on_id":"bd-1xoe","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:29Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3jxy","depends_on_id":"bd-2qcn","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:29Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3jxy","depends_on_id":"bd-36he","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:29Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3k4sh","title":"Task: Implement synthesis prompt templates and strategy instructions","description":"# Task: Implement synthesis prompt templates and strategy instructions

Provide templated prompts for the synthesizer agent, with strategy-specific instructions and structured rendering of mode outputs.

## Component Overview
Templates structure how collected ModeOutput data is presented to the synthesizer agent.

## Base Template
```
# Ensemble Synthesis Task

You are the SYNTHESIZER for a reasoning ensemble. Multiple agents have analyzed the problem using distinct reasoning modes. Your task is to synthesize their outputs into a coherent, actionable analysis.

## Problem Statement
{{.Problem}}

## Synthesis Strategy: {{.Strategy}}
{{.StrategyInstructions}}

## Agent Outputs
{{range .Outputs}}
### {{.ModeName}} ({{.ModeID}}{{if .ModeCode}} / {{.ModeCode}}{{end}})
{{.Content}}
---
{{end}}

## Your Task
{{.SynthesisInstructions}}
```

## Strategy-Specific Instructions
- Align with strategy definitions in bd-2ztw9 (manual, adversarial, consensus, creative, analytical, deliberative, prioritized, dialectical, meta-reasoning, voting, argumentation-graph).
- Manual/voting/argumentation strategies may bypass the synthesizer agent; template should still exist for agent-based variants when requested.
- Each strategy should define explicit focus and required output sections.

## Implementation
```go
// internal/ensemble/templates.go
type SynthesisTemplate struct {
    Name     string
    Strategy SynthesisStrategy
    Template *template.Template
}

func (t *SynthesisTemplate) Render(input *SynthesisInput) (string, error)
func LoadTemplates() map[SynthesisStrategy]*SynthesisTemplate
func GetTemplate(strategy SynthesisStrategy) (*SynthesisTemplate, error)
```

## File Location
internal/ensemble/templates.go

## Dependencies
- Strategy definitions (bd-2ztw9)
- Synthesis system feature (bd-2qwm8)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Base template renders without error
- [ ] All strategies have templates
- [ ] Templates include all mode outputs in stable order
- [ ] Errors surfaced with helpful context","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-23T07:39:55.160470750Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T07:57:37.462331499Z","closed_at":"2026-01-25T07:57:37.462311050Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3k4sh","depends_on_id":"bd-2qwm8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:29Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3k4sh","depends_on_id":"bd-2ztw9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:29Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-ok7rj","title":"Implement baseline capture for probe methods","description":"Implement baseline capture functionality that all probe methods rely on.

## Purpose

All probe methods need to capture pane content before and after sending probe input.
This task implements the shared baseline capture logic.

## Implementation

```go
type PaneBaseline struct {
    Content      string    // Full pane content
    ContentHash  string    // For quick comparison
    CursorLine   int       // If available from tmux
    CursorCol    int       // If available
    CapturedAt   time.Time
}

func capturePaneBaseline(session string, pane int) (*PaneBaseline, error) {
    // Use tmux capture-pane with cursor position if possible
    // Format: -p for content, -P for formatting info
    target := fmt.Sprintf("%s:1.%d", session, pane)
    
    // Capture content
    cmd := exec.Command("/usr/bin/tmux", "capture-pane", "-t", target, "-p")
    content, err := cmd.Output()
    if err != nil {
        return nil, fmt.Errorf("baseline capture failed: %w", err)
    }
    
    return &PaneBaseline{
        Content:     string(content),
        ContentHash: sha256Sum(content),
        CapturedAt:  time.Now(),
    }, nil
}

func comparePaneState(baseline, current *PaneBaseline) PaneChange {
    if baseline.ContentHash == current.ContentHash {
        return PaneChange{Changed: false}
    }
    
    // Compute diff for detailed analysis
    return PaneChange{
        Changed:    true,
        LinesDelta: countLineDiff(baseline.Content, current.Content),
    }
}
```

## Edge Cases

- Empty pane content (new session)
- Binary content in pane (rare)
- Very large pane content (truncation)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T23:44:20.334752523Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-20T18:41:44.792628036Z","closed_at":"2026-01-20T18:41:44.792582911Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-ok7rj","depends_on_id":"bd-1cu1f","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:31Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-p3en","title":"Task: Define command tier taxonomy and categorization","description":"## Overview
Create the foundational tier taxonomy that categorizes all NTM commands into proficiency levels.

## Deliverables
1. Create internal/cli/tiers/taxonomy.go defining:
   - Tier enum (Apprentice=1, Journeyman=2, Master=3)
   - CommandTier struct with {Command, MinTier, Description, Examples}
   - Global registry of all commands with their tier assignments

2. Tier 1 (Apprentice) - Essential workflow:
   - spawn: Create agent sessions
   - send: Send prompts to agents
   - status: View running sessions
   - kill: Stop sessions
   - help: Get help (tier-aware)

3. Tier 2 (Journeyman) - Full standard:
   - All Tier 1 commands
   - dashboard: Interactive TUI
   - context: View context window usage
   - tail: View agent output
   - interrupt: Send Ctrl+C to agents
   - swarm: Swarm orchestration
   - All standard flags

4. Tier 3 (Master) - Advanced/experimental:
   - All Tier 2 commands
   - --robot-* flags for automation
   - Internal debugging commands
   - Experimental features

## Implementation Notes
- Use Go constants for tier values
- Include help text per tier explaining what unlocks next
- Document promotion criteria in tier definitions

## Acceptance Criteria
- taxonomy.go compiles and exports tier constants
- All existing commands have tier assignments
- Tier progression is logical (each tier builds on previous)
- Unit tests verify taxonomy completeness","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T18:24:07.875431526Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-20T06:03:20.700671521Z","closed_at":"2026-01-20T06:03:20.700604966Z","close_reason":"Implemented command tier taxonomy in internal/cli/tiers/taxonomy.go with Tier enum, CommandInfo struct, and complete Registry mapping all commands","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-p3en","depends_on_id":"bd-7ny3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:31Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-p6quz","title":"Feature: Robot API - JSON interface for ensemble automation","description":"# Feature: Robot API

## Purpose
Expose ensemble functionality through JSON API for AI-to-AI orchestration and automation pipelines. Follows existing NTM robot mode patterns.

## Scope

### Robot Commands

**--robot-ensemble=SESSION**
Get ensemble state for a session.
```json
{
  "success": true,
  "timestamp": "2026-01-23T10:30:00Z",
  "ensemble": {
    "session": "myproject",
    "question": "What architectural problems exist?",
    "preset": "architecture-review",
    "status": "active",
    "started_at": "2026-01-23T10:25:00Z",
    "modes": [
      {"id": "abductive", "code": "B13", "tier": "advanced", "pane": "myproject__cc_1", "agent_type": "claude", "status": "done"}
    ],
    "synthesis": {"strategy": "deliberative", "status": "not_started"}
  },
  "summary": {"total_modes": 5, "completed": 2, "working": 2, "pending": 1}
}
```

**--robot-ensemble-spawn=SESSION**
Create new ensemble session.
```bash
ntm --robot-ensemble-spawn=myproject \
  --preset=architecture-review \
  --question='What problems exist?' \
  --agents=cc=3,cod=2

ntm --robot-ensemble-spawn=myproject \
  --modes=A1,B13,F43 \
  --allow-advanced=true \
  --question='...'
```

**--robot-ensemble-synthesize=SESSION**
Trigger synthesis.
```bash
ntm --robot-ensemble-synthesize=myproject \
  --strategy=deliberative \
  --format=markdown
```
Returns synthesis output metadata or status.

**--robot-ensemble-modes**
List available modes.
```json
{
  "success": true,
  "modes": [
    {"id": "abductive", "code": "B13", "tier": "advanced", "name": "Abductive Reasoning", "category": {"code": "B", "name": "Ampliative"}}
  ],
  "categories": [{"code": "A", "name": "Formal", "mode_count": 8}],
  "default_tier": "core",
  "total_modes": 80,
  "core_modes": 28,
  "advanced_modes": 52,
  "experimental_modes": 0
}
```

**--robot-ensemble-presets**
List available ensembles (presets).
```json
{
  "success": true,
  "presets": [
    {"name": "architecture-review", "display": "Architecture Review", "description": "...", "modes": [{"id":"systems-thinking","code":"F43"}], "synthesis": {"strategy": "deliberative"}, "budget": {"total": 30000}, "allow_advanced": false, "tags": ["architecture"]}
  ]
}
```

### Modifiers (Unprefixed)
- **Spawn:** `--question`, `--preset`, `--modes`, `--agents`, `--assignment`, `--allow-advanced`, `--budget-total`, `--budget-per-agent`, `--no-cache`, `--no-questions`, `--project`
- **Modes list:** `--category`, `--tier`, `--limit`, `--offset`
- **Synthesize:** `--strategy`, `--format`, `--output`, `--force`

### Strategy Set
Uses canonical set from bd-2ztw9 (manual, adversarial, consensus, creative, analytical, deliberative, prioritized, dialectical, meta-reasoning, voting, argumentation-graph).

### Exit Codes
- 0: Success
- 1: Error (with error_code in JSON)
- 2: Feature unavailable

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All robot commands return valid JSON
- [ ] Follows existing robot envelope pattern (success/timestamp, optional error fields on failure)
- [ ] Exit codes are consistent
- [ ] Arrays initialized to [] not nil
- [ ] Error codes are documented
- [ ] _agent_hints included where helpful

## Technical Notes
- Follow patterns in internal/robot/
- Add to capabilities.go for discoverability
- Ensure envelope compliance (see envelope_test.go)
- Modifiers are **unprefixed** per docs/robot-api-design.md","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-23T07:26:15.832578580Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-23T20:12:58.509021100Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-p6quz","depends_on_id":"bd-12u4u","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:31Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-p6quz","depends_on_id":"bd-49fb9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:31Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-pjjk7","title":"EPIC: Canonical Assignment Flow (ntm assign + spawn --assign)","description":"## Goal
Reduce user confusion by providing **one** canonical assignment flow and **one** convenience entry point.

## Decision
- **Keep**: `ntm assign` as the only assignment command.
- **Keep**: `ntm spawn --assign` as the only convenience entry (spawn + assign).
- **Expose**: `--strategy=balanced|speed|quality|dependency|round-robin` on `ntm assign`.
- **Drop/Merge**: `ntm swarm`, `ntm send --distribute`, YAML swarm config, and from‑file assignments.

## Why
Multiple entry points doing the same thing (assign work to agents) multiplies docs, tests, and code paths and will confuse users. The project benefits from a single, well‑designed path.

## Scope
- Keep existing strategies (`balanced`, `speed`, `quality`, `dependency`) intact and documented.
- Add `round‑robin` as an **optional** additional strategy (no renaming of existing ones).
- Implement assignment tracking + reporting so `balanced` can be truly load‑aware.
- Implement `ntm spawn --assign` as a thin wrapper over `ntm assign`.
- Update docs/help to emphasize canonical flow.

## Non‑Goals
- No new orchestration commands outside `assign` + `spawn --assign`.
- No YAML swarm config or additional CLI entry points.

## Success Criteria
- `ntm assign` is the only path for distributing work.
- `ntm spawn --assign` works end‑to‑end with clear output.
- Redundant commands are closed/dropped.
- Strategy list in docs/help matches code.","notes":"## Testing Requirements
- Unit tests: core logic, error paths, edge cases.
- E2E scripts: full CLI/robot flow with detailed log/slog prefixes (e.g., [E2E-<AREA>]) and include session/agent/bead IDs.
- If structured outputs are affected, assert schema stability + deterministic ordering.

+package webhook
+
+import (
+	"crypto/hmac"
+	"crypto/sha256"
+	"encoding/hex"
+	"encoding/json"
+	"io"
+	"net/http"
+	"net/http/httptest"
+	"sync"
+	"testing"
+	"time"
+)
+
+func TestQueueOverflow_DropsOldest(t *testing.T) {
+	t.Parallel()
+
+	var (
+		mu       sync.Mutex
+		received []Event
+	)
+
+	block := make(chan struct{})
+	firstStarted := make(chan struct{}, 1)
+
+	ts := httptest.NewServer(http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
+		body, _ := io.ReadAll(r.Body)
+		var ev Event
+		_ = json.Unmarshal(body, &ev)
+
+		mu.Lock()
+		received = append(received, ev)
+		n := len(received)
+		mu.Unlock()
+
+		if n == 1 {
+			// Block the first delivery so the worker can't drain the queue.
+			select {
+			case firstStarted <- struct{}{}:
+			default:
+			}
+			<-block
+		}
+
+		w.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)
+	}))
+	defer ts.Close()
+
+	mgr := NewManager(ManagerConfig{
+		QueueSize:   1,
+		WorkerCount: 1,
+	})
+	if err := mgr.Register(WebhookConfig{
+		ID:      "test",
+		URL:     ts.URL,
+		Enabled: true,
+	}); err != nil {
+		t.Fatalf("register: %v", err)
+	}
+	if err := mgr.Start(); err != nil {
+		t.Fatalf("start: %v", err)
+	}
+	defer mgr.Stop()
+
+	if err := mgr.Dispatch(Event{Type: "test.queue", Message: "first"}); err != nil {
+		t.Fatalf("dispatch first: %v", err)
+	}
+
+	select {
+	case <-firstStarted:
+	case <-time.After(5 * time.Second):
+		t.Fatalf("timeout waiting for first delivery to start")
+	}
+
+	// This will sit in the queue while the worker is blocked on the first delivery.
+	_ = mgr.Dispatch(Event{Type: "test.queue", Message: "second"})
+	// This should force a queue overflow and drop the queued "second" delivery.
+	_ = mgr.Dispatch(Event{Type: "test.queue", Message: "third"})
+
+	if mgr.Stats().DroppedEvents == 0 {
+		close(block)
+		t.Fatalf("expected dropped events when queue is full")
+	}
+
+	close(block)
+
+	deadline := time.Now().Add(5 * time.Second)
+	for time.Now().Before(deadline) {
+		mu.Lock()
+		n := len(received)
+		mu.Unlock()
+		if n >= 2 {
+			break
+		}
+		time.Sleep(10 * time.Millisecond)
+	}
+
+	mu.Lock()
+	got := append([]Event(nil), received...)
+	mu.Unlock()
+
+	if len(got) != 2 {
+		t.Fatalf("expected 2 deliveries (first + newest), got %d", len(got))
+	}
+	if got[0].Message != "first" {
+		t.Fatalf("expected first delivery to be first, got %q", got[0].Message)
+	}
+	if got[1].Message != "third" {
+		t.Fatalf("expected overflow to keep newest delivery (third), got %q", got[1].Message)
+	}
+}
+
+func TestHMACSignature_Deterministic(t *testing.T) {
+	t.Parallel()
+
+	m := NewManager(DefaultManagerConfig())
+	secret := "test-secret"
+	payload := []byte(`{"hello":"world"}`)
+
+	got1 := m.sign(payload, secret)
+	got2 := m.sign(payload, secret)
+	if got1 != got2 {
+		t.Fatalf("expected deterministic signature, got %q vs %q", got1, got2)
+	}
+
+	mac := hmac.New(sha256.New, []byte(secret))
+	_, _ = mac.Write(payload)
+	want := hex.EncodeToString(mac.Sum(nil))
+	if got1 != want {
+		t.Fatalf("expected signature %q, got %q", want, got1)
+	}
+
+	gotOther := m.sign(payload, "different-secret")
+	if gotOther == got1 {
+		t.Fatalf("expected signature to differ with different secret")
+	}
+}
